response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering experience across web, mobile, and some embedded-adjacent work, but the role requires native-level Japanese proficiency (not listed), strong C for UNIX with Linux kernel modification experience (only brief C/C++ exposure at NI a decade ago), and embedded networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips) which is absent from the resume. The mismatch on hard requirements is decisive.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with 15+ years of delivery experience
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang, C/C++ at National Instruments, and Linux tooling
Demonstrated ability to learn new domains and own modules end-to-end
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement is native-level)
No Linux kernel source modification or embedded firmware/switch experience
No security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP)
